answer:
The argument has premise ~K ≡ L and conclusion (K · L) ∨ (K · ~L).  
The conclusion simplifies to K · (L ∨ ~L) which is just K, so the argument asks whether ~K ≡ L logically implies K.  
This is invalid because when K is false and L is true, the premise is true but the conclusion is false.  

\boxed{D}
